---
title: "PCN — Projects"
---

[← PCN notes](index.html)   ·   [← Back to portal](/index.html)


The project work for **PCN** lives in its own repository.

[<i class="bi bi-github"></i>&nbsp; Open the PCN projects on GitHub](https://github.com/PhyAMR/PCN-projects){.btn .btn-outline-dark .btn-lg role="button"}

<https://github.com/PhyAMR/PCN-projects>


---

## `theoretical_projects/topic_01_statistical_mechanics_on_complex_networks/`

### Topic 1. Statistical mechanics on complex networks

The objective of this project is to simulate the behavior of some well-known models, and possibly analyze their critical behavior (i.e., how the order parameter changes with the control parameter). The students are left free to follow the bibliographic references to understand what should be simulated and how, as well as to reproduce the main results and, if willing to do so, explore new directions. It is required that these models are studied, computationally, across a variety of different synthetic topologies. Applications to real-world networks will be a plus.

---

## `theoretical_projects/topic_02_growth_models_with_structural_phase_transitions_i/`

### Topic 2. Growth models with structural phase transitions I

The objective of this project is to simulate the behavior of some well-known growth models, and possibly analyze their critical behavior (e.g., the emergence of a giant connected component for varying control parameter(s) of each model). The students are left free to follow the bibliographic references to understand what should be simulated and how, as well as to reproduce the main results and, if willing to do so, explore new directions.

---

## `theoretical_projects/topic_03_growth_models_with_structural_phase_transitions_ii/`

### Topic 3. Growth models with structural phase transitions II

The objective of this project is to simulate the behavior of some well-known growth models, and possibly analyze their critical behavior (e.g., the emergence of a giant connected component for varying control parameter(s) of each model). The students are left free to follow the bibliographic references to understand what should be simulated and how, as well as to reproduce the main results and, if willing to do so, explore new directions.

---

## `theoretical_projects/topic_04_growth_models_with_structural_phase_transitions_iii/`

### Topic 4. Growth models with structural phase transitions III

The objective of this project is to simulate the behavior of some well-known growth models, and possibly analyze their critical behavior (e.g., the emergence of a giant connected component for varying control parameter(s) of each model). The students are left free to follow the bibliographic references to understand what should be simulated and how, as well as to reproduce the main results and, if willing to do so, explore new directions.

---

## `theoretical_projects/topic_05_cascade_failures/`

### Topic 5. Cascade failures 

The objective of this project is to simulate the behavior of some well-known dynamical models, and possibly analyze their critical behavior (e.g., the breakdown of the largest connected component for varying control parameter(s) of each model). The students are left free to follow the bibliographic references to understand what should be simulated and how, as well as to reproduce the main results and, if willing to do so, explore new directions.

---

## `theoretical_projects/topic_06_dynamics_on_networks/`

### Topic 6. Dynamics on networks

The objective of this project is to simulate the behavior of some well-known dynamical models, and possibly analyze their behavior (e.g., their parameter space or their dependence on targeted perturbations). The students are left free to follow the bibliographic references to understand what should be simulated and how, as well as to reproduce the main results and, if willing to do so, explore new directions.

---

## `theoretical_projects/topic_07_dynamics_on_networks_ii/`

### Topic 7. Dynamics on networks II

The objective of this project is to simulate the behavior of some well-known dynamical models, and possibly analyze their behavior (e.g., identify the critical epidemic threshold or the invasion threshold). The students are left free to follow the bibliographic references to understand what should be simulated and how, as well as to reproduce the main results and, if willing to do so, explore new directions.

---

## `theoretical_projects/topic_08_quantum_networks/`

### Topic 8. Quantum networks

The objective of this project is to simulate the behavior of some known dynamical models for quantum networks. The students are left free to follow the bibliographic references to understand what should be simulated and how, as well as to reproduce the main results and, if willing to do so, explore new directions.

---

## `theoretical_projects/topic_09_sociophysics_i/`

### Topic 9. Sociophysics I

The objective of this project is to simulate some well known dynamical models of Sociophysics, and possibly analyze their behavior (e.g., the influence of the dynamical parameters and network topological traits on the behavior of the model). For the voter model, it is enough to simulate and understand its classical version and see how the properties change when variations in the update rules are considered. The students are left free to follow the bibliographic references to understand what should be simulated and how, as well as to reproduce the main results and, if willing to do so, explore new directions.

---

## `theoretical_projects/topic_10_sociophysics_ii/`

### Topic 10. Sociophysics II

The objective of this project is to simulate some well known dynamical models of Sociophysics, and possibly analyze their behavior (e.g., the influence of the dynamical parameters and network topological traits on the behavior of the model). The students are left free to follow the bibliographic references to understand what should be simulated and how, as well as to reproduce the main results and, if willing to do so, explore new directions.

---

## `theoretical_projects/topic_11_sociophysics_iii/`

### Topic 11. Sociophysics III

The objective of this project is to simulate some well known dynamical models of Sociophysics, and possibly analyze their behavior (e.g., the influence of the dynamical parameters and network topological traits on the behavior of the model). The students are left free to follow the bibliographic references to understand what should be simulated and how, as well as to reproduce the main results and, if willing to do so, explore new directions.

---

## `theoretical_projects/topic_12_percolation/`

### Topic 12. Percolation

The objective of this project is to simulate the percolation process for different removing protocols, and characterize the phase space and the critical properties (e.g., the size of the giant component, the distribution of small clusters, assessing whether the transition of first/second/hybrid order, etc.) The students are left free to follow the bibliographic references to understand what should be simulated and how, as well as to reproduce the main results and, if willing to do so, explore new directions.
